question:
The performance of a manufacturing system was analyzed over 24 hours, noting that if:
a. 220 parts come in, 220 parts come out.
b. 270 parts come in, 270 parts come out.
c. 378 parts enter, 340 parts come out.
d. 341 parts enter, 340 parts come out.
In which of the 4 scenarios, presented above, the system works optimally, i.e. the system works at maximum capacity and no raw parts remain unprocessed?
For 1 products of type P1, with the structure shown below, how many M6 units are required? Calculations:

Taking into account the data presented in the table below calculates the value of stocks at the end of December and writes in the table the obtained value:
Month | Sales (mil.of €) [S_i] | Labor (hr) [L_i] | Days worked [d_i] | Level workforce (number of employees required) [E_i] | Monthly production (millions of dollars) [mp_i] | Inventory balance (mil. of €) [Ib_i]
Nov | 16 | 80000 | 20 | 809 | 12.94 | 3.60
Dec | 11.6 | 58000 | 15 | 809 | 9.6 | 
Planned work week is 40 hours long, Sales euros estimated/employee/hr is €100
Calculations:
Which of the following actions are not specific to sustainable production?

options.B: increasing the level of utilization of an equipment

options.A: planning the equipment and the labor needed to accomplish the production plan
options.B: how many of each type to sell
options.C: when the products should be completed

question: When the design for life cycle is considered:

options.A: the production factors are taken into account
options.B: the customer's wishes will be satisfied or exceeded
options.C: factors ranging from product delivery to product disposal are considered

question:
At the beginning of one year, a company decided that it should maintain a safety stock of 1.3 million euros for the next 12 months. Taking into account the records presented in the table below for the last 4 months of the first year, does the company comply with the established level of safety stocks?
Month | Sales (mil. of €) [S_i] | Labor (hr) [L_i] | Days worked [d_i] | Level workforce (number of employees required) [E_i] | Monthly production (millions of dollars) [mp_i] | Inventory balance (mil. of €) [Ib_i]
Sept | 29.6 | 148000 | 21 | 684 | 22.98 | 12.15
Oct | 24.8 | 124000 | 22 | 684 | 24.08 | 11.43
Nov | 32 | 160000 | 20 | 684 | 21.89 | 1.32
Dec | 22.2 | 111000 | 20 | 684 | 21.89 | ?
Planned work week is 40 hours long, Sales euros estimated/employee/hr is €200

options.A: No, because except for December, for the other months the level of stocks is higher than that of the safety stock.
options.B: Yes, because the average stock level for the last 4 months is much higher than the safety stock.
options.C: No, because the stock level at the end of December is lower than the safety stock.
options.D: Yes, because the stock level for each month is higher than that of the safety stock.
